title: acmella decumbens
intro: Acmella decumbens, also known as toothache plant, is a tropical plant species in the sunflower family, native to the Americas, characterized by its small yellow flowers and edible leaves. The plant is traditionally used for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a popular remedy for toothaches, gum inflammation, and other oral health issues. The health benefits of Acmella decumbens include pain relief, reduced swelling, and antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent infections and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
